title: ruta chalepensis proles bracteosa
intro: Ruta chalepensis proles bracteosa, also known as Syrian rue, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and lance-shaped leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its emetic and purgative effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, specifically sanguinarine. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including treating parasitic infections, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Ruta chalepensis proles bracteosa is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its roots are used in traditional medicine, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
